sections.paragraph:
μυστῆριον, ου, τό [μύω ‘be shut’, cp. μυέω] ‘that which awaits disclosure or interpretation’, mystery, secret

—a. of divine counsel and plans that have awaited disclosure and interpretation Mt 13:11; Ro 11:25; 16:25; 1 Cor 2:1, 7; 4:1; 13:2; 15:51; Eph 1:9; 3:3f, 9; 6:19; Col 1:27; Rv 10:7.

—b. of special truth interpreted or in need of interpretation 1 Cor 14:2; Eph 5:32; 1 Ti 3:16; Rv 1:20; 17:5, 7.

—c. in association with a phenomenon whose nature awaits disclosure 2 Th 2:7.

—d. as climactic substance of faith 1 Ti 3:9.
